artifacts.name: Trial Master File Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe how records for the trial will be managed and stored during and after the trial, including study-specific processes and documentation for archiving and destruction. To include TMF filing s

artifacts.name: Trial Management Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe overall strategy for timelines, management and conduct of the trial and typically makes reference to other artifacts. Artifact can include details on contingency plan covering details for

artifacts.name: Quality Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the operational techniques and activities undertaken within the quality management system to verify that the requirements for quality of the trial-related activities have been fulfilled. R

artifacts.name: List of SOPs Current During Trial
artifacts.definition: To document which standard operating procedures (SOPs) and which versions were in effect for the duration of the trial and trial-specific procedures created for the trial. To include sponsor and third

artifacts.name: Operational Procedure Manual
artifacts.definition: To describe trial-related processes not covered by formal standard operating procedures. Includes manuals given to sites for ISFs and vendor study-specific manuals as well as any study related tools p

artifacts.name: Recruitment Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the planned subject enrolment/recruitment goals during the trial, including contingency plans. Artifact can include any evidence of plan execution including, but not limited to: plan, repo

artifacts.name: Communication Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe communication strategy and plans between trial stakeholders, including communication escalation procedures/steps. Artifact can include any evidence of plan execution including, but not lim

artifacts.name: Monitoring Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe how monitoring will be implemented during the trial, including strategy for source data verification and risk based monitoring (if applicable). Artifact can include any trial level evidenc

artifacts.name: Medical Monitoring Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe how medical surveillance of trial subjects will be assured during the trial. Artifact can include any evidence of plan execution including, but not limited to: plan, reports, checklists, e

artifacts.name: Publication Policy
artifacts.definition: To describe the policy for publishing the trial results if publication policy is not captured within the protocol.

artifacts.name: Debarment Statement
artifacts.definition: To verify whether the applicant or any of its principals is currently debarred, suspended, proposed for debarment, or declared ineligible to receive federal awards; whether within the past three years

artifacts.name: Trial Status Report
artifacts.definition: Routine trial status progress report generated by the sponsor or 3rd Party and distributed to trial stakeholders.

artifacts.name: Investigator Newsletter
artifacts.definition: To inform investigative staff of common implementation issues and of the progress of the trial.

artifacts.name: Audit Certificate
artifacts.definition: Documentation to confirm that an audit was performed (does not contain the audit report).

artifacts.name: Filenote Master List
artifacts.definition: To provide a consolidated list/index of file notes generated during the trial.

artifacts.name: Risk Management Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the potential hazards associated with the trial, including an assessment of the likelihood of those hazards occurring and resulting in harm. The Risk Management Plan is intended to include

artifacts.name: Vendor Management Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the overall management strategy for third party vendors used to conduct trial-related activities. May include assignment of responsibilities for third party vendor oversight, performance i

artifacts.name: Roles and Responsibility Matrix
artifacts.definition: To identify range and distribution of tasks and responsibilities; may define internal assignment and all external parties; covers GCP as well as business process; often part of the Contractual Agreeme

artifacts.name: Transfer of Regulatory Obligations
artifacts.definition: To specify the transfer of regulatory obligations from sponsor to each Affiliate/CRO/Vendor and may include other agreements. A sponsor may transfer responsibility for any or all of the obligations se

artifacts.name: Operational Oversight
artifacts.definition: Documentation to show evidence of sponsor oversight of study, as well as any key decisions taken and the supporting rationale. Records demonstrating oversight of a specific third party should be filed

artifacts.name: Trial Team Details
artifacts.definition: To define trial roles, contact details and structure of the trial team - both sponsor and third parties; optionally this may include full and initials-only signature of all team members, role-to-role

artifacts.name: Trial Team Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of sponsor trial team members. Documentation for third party trial team members should be filed in Zone 09.

artifacts.name: Committee Process
artifacts.definition: To describe the purpose and mode of operation/manner of working of the Independent Trial Committee, which may be established by the sponsor to assess at intervals the progress of a clinical trial the

artifacts.name: Committee Member List
artifacts.definition: To document the current composition of a trial committee. Can be part of the Charter.

artifacts.name: Committee Output
artifacts.definition: To document any agreements or significant decisions regarding trial conduct, protocol violations, adverse event reporting, to include minutes, reports, notifications, recommendations from a trial comm

artifacts.name: Committee Member Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of the Committee Member to provide assessments, at set intervals, the progress of a clinical trial, of the safety data and the critical efficacy endpoints an

artifacts.name: Committee Member Financial Disclosure Form
artifacts.definition: To certify that no financial arrangements with a Committee Member have been made where study outcome could affect compensation; that the Committee Member has no proprietary interest in the tested prod

artifacts.name: Committee Member Contract
artifacts.definition: To document agreement of trial requirements between sponsor or 3rd Party and Committee Member.

artifacts.name: Committee Member Confidentiality Disclosure Agreement
artifacts.definition: A document between the sponsor and the Committee Member that defines the terms and basic criteria to assure that the party (or parties) receiving confidential information will maintain confidentiality

artifacts.name: Kick-off Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation made available for attendees of the trial kick-off meeting, including attendance sheets. Does not include Investigator Meeting content.

artifacts.name: Trial Team Training Material
artifacts.definition: Trial-relevant training materials , including use of specialized systems.

artifacts.name: Investigators Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation made available for attendees of the investigator meeting(s). Includes meeting minutes or questions and answers (Q&A), attendance sheets and any p

artifacts.name: Trial Team Evidence of Training
artifacts.definition: To document completion of study-specific trial team training, including certification or evidence of training (attendance sheets). Includes EDC training. Does not include each individual's education,

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but are not limited to: letters, me

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Other Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during any other internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone. Filenotes referencing general topics and/or topics across multiple zones may be  filed within this zone.

artifacts.name: Investigator's Brochure
artifacts.definition: To provide relevant and current clinical and non-clinical data on the investigational product(s) that is related to the study of the product(s) in human subjects. The Investigational Medicinal Product

artifacts.name: Protocol
artifacts.definition: To describe the objective(s), design, methodology, statistical considerations and organization of a trial. Usually also gives the background and rationale for the trial, but these could also be provid

artifacts.name: Protocol Synopsis
artifacts.definition: A summary of the pertinent points of the protocol. A local language version may be translated from core (English) or produced in the country if required by local Regulatory Authorities or IRB/IEC

artifacts.name: Protocol Amendment
artifacts.definition: Subsequent versions of the original protocol as well as supporting documentation that may include description of change(s) to or formal clarification of a protocol. Includes justification for a non-su

artifacts.name: Financial Disclosure Summary
artifacts.definition: Summary documentation of compliance with financial disclosure reporting requirements, per company and/or local government policies.  May include summaries, lists, other reports.

artifacts.name: Insurance
artifacts.definition: To document that compensation to subject(s) for trial-related injury will be available. May include policy and certificates, terms and conditions. Certificate is core, policy is recommended.

artifacts.name: Sample Case Report Form
artifacts.definition: Blank forms / templates in paper form or e-Format to capture the data points of the protocol.

artifacts.name: Report of Prior Investigations
artifacts.definition: To include reports of all prior clinical, animal and laboratory testing of the device and shall be comprehensive and adequate to justify the proposed investigation. Can be in addition or instead of an

artifacts.name: Marketed Product Material
artifacts.definition: Materials available in the legal pharmacologic description of a drug or device, subject to detailed regulatory specifications, including approved chemical and proprietary names, description and classi

artifacts.name: Subject Diary
artifacts.definition: To document subject data captured by the subject and external to the CRF (blank forms / templates).

artifacts.name: Subject Questionnaire
artifacts.definition: To capture specific subject related information through a series of questions (blank forms / templates)

artifacts.name: Informed Consent Form
artifacts.definition: To document that the appropriate written information (content and wording) has been given to subjects regarding the trial to support their ability to give fully informed consent and to document their

artifacts.name: Subject Information Sheet
artifacts.definition: To document information provided to subjects to support their decision about whether or not to participate in the trial.

artifacts.name: Subject Participation Card
artifacts.definition: To be provided to the subject to carry to document trial participation (blank template).

artifacts.name: Advertisements for Subject Recruitment
artifacts.definition: Materials used in clinical trial recruitment campaigns; approved by the IRB/IEC to ensure recruitment measures are appropriate and not coercive.

artifacts.name: Other Information Given to Subjects
artifacts.definition: Materials provided to the subject to further assist with understanding the trial requirements or concepts; may include memory aids or retention materials.

artifacts.name: Clinical Study Report
artifacts.definition: To describe final or interim results and interpretation of trial of any therapeutic, prophylactic, or diagnostic agent conducted in human subjects, in which all Clinical Study Report components are in

artifacts.name: Bioanalytical Report
artifacts.definition: To present & summarize the relevant top line findings of the bioanalytical aspects of the interim or final analysis and may include PK analysis or reports.

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Regulatory Submission
artifacts.definition: A set of documents, along with required associated regulatory forms and correspondence, submitted to one or more regulatory agencies requesting approval to conduct the trial or for the purpose of noti

artifacts.name: Regulatory Authority Decision
artifacts.definition: A documented notification received from a regulatory authority stating that the Submission has been received and approved.

artifacts.name: Notification of Regulatory Identification Number
artifacts.definition: Document identifying unique Identification (ID) number used to uniquely identify the trial or the trial level in that region, assigned by a regulatory agency – e.g. EU = EudraCT Number, FDA = IND Numb

artifacts.name: Public Registration
artifacts.definition: Documentation related to registration of clinical trials in public registries such as ClinicalTrials.gov and to submission of results periodically during the study and at study completion.

artifacts.name: Import or Export License Application
artifacts.definition: An application made to one or more regulatory agencies requesting a license to import or export the investigational product and clinical supplies.

artifacts.name: Import or Export Documentation
artifacts.definition: A document issued by a national government authorizing the importation or exportation of certain goods into its territory.

artifacts.name: Notification of Safety or Trial Information
artifacts.definition: Notification to Regulatory Authorities of any trial events that could alter the regulatory authority's approval/favorable opinion to continue the trial. Notifications may include, but are not limited

artifacts.name: Regulatory Progress Report
artifacts.definition: Reports concerning trial conduct, progress and status that are required to be periodically submitted to relevant regulatory authorities. This artifact is not intended to include safety reports require

artifacts.name: Regulatory Notification of Trial Termination
artifacts.definition: Document detailing the termination of a trial – whether upon completion or premature termination.

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

4.name: IRB or IEC and other Approvals

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Submission
artifacts.definition: Documents describing the trial or changes/updates to the trial submitted to an IRB/IEC for approval, including recruitment and education materials and responses to questions from IRB/IEC to support a

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Decision
artifacts.definition: Documentation received from IRB/IEC in response to submission indicating decision of the trial and any specifications or modifications. Records referenced by the approval (such as a Protocol that has

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Composition
artifacts.definition: Documentation that the IRB/IEC consists of a reasonable number of members who collectively have the qualifications and experience to review and evaluate the science, medical aspects and ethics of the

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Documentation of Non-Voting Status
artifacts.definition: Documentation verifying non-voting members of the IRB/IEC if the investigator or sub-investigator is on the IRB/IEC.

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Compliance Documentation
artifacts.definition: Documentation that the IRB/IEC is performing its function according to written operating procedures and is in compliance with GCP and applicable regulatory requirements.

artifacts.name: Other Submissions
artifacts.definition:
A set of documents describing the trial or changes/updates to the trial submitted to a committee other than the IRB/IEC for approval. To include: Submissions and Correspondence 

The submitted study d

artifacts.name: Other Approvals
artifacts.definition: Approval documentation received from a committee other than the IRB/IEC in response to submission indicating approval/acknowledgement of trial specifications or modifications. To include: Submissions

artifacts.name: Notification to IRB or IEC of Safety Information
artifacts.definition: To assure the IRB/IEC are promptly notified of all findings (new, important information on serious adverse events and or safety concerns) that could adversely affect the safety of subjects, impact the

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Progress Report
artifacts.definition: Regular reports concerning trial conduct, other than safety reports, issued to the IRB/IEC by the sponsor/3rd Party and/or investigator.

artifacts.name: IRB or IEC Notification of Trial Termination
artifacts.definition: Document detailing the termination of a trial – whether upon completion or premature termination.

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Site Contact Details
artifacts.definition: To document contact information for primary points of contact at the site (e.g. Principal Investigator, Institution Name, Trial Coordinator, Contracts Person, etc.).

artifacts.name: Confidentiality Agreement
artifacts.definition: A document between the sponsor and an outside party (Investigator or Institution) that defines the terms and basic criteria to assure that the party (or parties) receiving confidential information wil

artifacts.name: Feasibility Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document site feasibility for the given protocol.

artifacts.name: Pre Trial Monitoring Report
artifacts.definition: To document onsite visit to determine qualification of site to participate in the trial. For example may include the following documentation: EDC qualification, Confirmation Letters / Emails, site pro

artifacts.name: Sites Evaluated but not Selected
artifacts.definition: Documentation related to sites evaluated but not selected for the trial.

artifacts.name: Acceptance of Investigator Brochure
artifacts.definition: To document that relevant and current scientific information about the investigational product has been provided to the investigator.

artifacts.name: Protocol Signature Page
artifacts.definition: To document investigator and sponsor agreement to the protocol.

artifacts.name: Protocol Amendment Signature Page
artifacts.definition: To document investigator and sponsor agreement to the protocol amendment.

artifacts.name: Principal Investigator Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of the Principal Investigator to conduct trial and/or provide medical supervision of subjects. To include updates, one-page CVs and biographical sketches.

artifacts.name: Sub-Investigator Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of any sub-Investigators to conduct trial and/or provide medical supervision of subjects. Sub-Investigators include any individual member of the clinical tri

artifacts.name: Other Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of site personnel other than the Principal Investigator or Sub-Investigators to conduct trial and/or provide medical supervision of subjects.

artifacts.name: Site Staff Qualification Supporting Information
artifacts.definition: To document site staff qualifications not previously outlined on CVs. May include list of studies, publications, training certificates for specific examinations, ICH-GCP training, site GCP or trial li

artifacts.name: Form FDA 1572
artifacts.definition: For IND trial, 1572 must be completed globally for FDA submission.

artifacts.name: Investigator Regulatory Agreement
artifacts.definition: A regulatory statement from the investigator required by certain health authorities e.g. includes but is not limited to ‘Qualified Investigator Undertaking’ form and ‘Clinical Trial Site Information’

artifacts.name: Financial Disclosure Form
artifacts.definition: To document financial disclosures, certification documentation and conflicts of interest, which include but are not limited to: completed disclosure forms of financial interests and arrangements of cl

artifacts.name: Data Privacy Agreement
artifacts.definition: To document agreement between sponsor and Site Staff (e.g., national or regional data privacy requirements); often contained in Clinical Trial Agreement

artifacts.name: Clinical Trial Agreement
artifacts.definition: To document agreement of trial requirements between sponsor or 3rd Party and site/ PI. Includes indemnity unless separate document created.

artifacts.name: Indemnity
artifacts.definition: To provide legal protection "as required by country regulations" in the event of an unforeseen adverse circumstance arising during the course of a clinical trial. May be in Clinical Trial Agreement

artifacts.name: Other Financial Agreement
artifacts.definition: To document agreement of trial requirements between other parties involved in the conduct of the trial. Includes indemnity unless separate document created.

artifacts.name: IP Site Release Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document approval for sites to receive drug supply / investigational product.

artifacts.name: Site Signature Sheet
artifacts.definition: To document delegation by the Principal Investigator of trial specific tasks to site personnel conducting the trial.

artifacts.name: Investigators Agreement (Device)
artifacts.definition: Non-financial agreement between the sponsor and the investigator documenting the various responsibilities, as outlined in CFR Title 21 part 812 as well as ICH-E6 (if applicable), in which the investig

artifacts.name: Coordinating Investigator Documentation
artifacts.definition: Documentation to show the approval of a coordinating investigator for a specific region or group of investigators that is not already captured as another artifact for that investigator. Documentation

artifacts.name: Trial Initiation Monitoring Report
artifacts.definition: To document that trial procedures were reviewed with the investigator and the trial personnel and confirm the site meets requirements to begin trial participation. trial initiation can be conducted vi

artifacts.name: Site Training Material
artifacts.definition: Training materials used to train the sites. Materials may be related to, but not limited to, Electronic Data Capture (EDC), Interactive Response Technology (IRT), Rater training. (Also includes traini

artifacts.name: Site Evidence of Training
artifacts.definition: To document completion of site training by relevant site personnel. Documentation includes attendance and certification for training delivered which may include Electronic Data Capture (EDC), Interac

artifacts.name: Subject Log
artifacts.definition: To anonymously list all subjects including screened, screen failures and enrolled for the sponsor.

artifacts.name: Source Data Verification
artifacts.definition: To document source data and associated verification activity

artifacts.name: Monitoring Visit Report
artifacts.definition: To document site visits, monitoring trial conduct and compliance of the site, may include confirmation letters/emails.

artifacts.name: Visit Log
artifacts.definition: To document monitoring visit dates and attendees.

artifacts.name: Additional Monitoring Activity
artifacts.definition: To document additional sponsor and/or study-specific monitoring activities.

artifacts.name: Protocol Deviations
artifacts.definition: To document non-compliance/ deviations to the protocol. This may also be a consolidated list for a country filed at the country level or a consolidated list for the study filed at the study level.

artifacts.name: Financial Documentation
artifacts.definition: Includes all invoices, receipts, payment summaries relating to the trial

artifacts.name: Final Trial Close Out Monitoring Report
artifacts.definition: To document trial activities are completed for site closure prior to trial completion. may include confirmation letters/emails.

artifacts.name: Notification to Investigators of Safety Information
artifacts.definition: To assure investigators are promptly notified of all findings (new, important information on serious adverse events and or safety concerns) that could adversely affect the safety of subjects, impact t

artifacts.name: Subject Identification Log
artifacts.definition: To fully identify all subjects screened, screen failed and enrolled in the trial, with unique institution identifiers where relevant

artifacts.name: Source Data
artifacts.definition: To document and confirm source data information at the Investigator site (i.e. medical records containing history of subjects).

artifacts.name: Monitoring Visit Follow-up Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document site visit follow-up. Could be grouped with monitoring visit reports.

artifacts.name: Subject Eligibility Verification Forms and Worksheets
artifacts.definition: Eligibility forms for qualification of trial subjects, may include inclusion/exclusion criteria, lab reports, doctor notes and other qualifying data usually used by (site) staff to ensure subjects are

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: IP Supply Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the following as they pertain to the IP: 1) quantity and packaging of active, placebo and/or if applicable, comparator or rescue supplies needed to fulfill the requirements of the trial pr

artifacts.name: IP Instructions for Handling
artifacts.definition: To instruct on how the IP should be handled during transit and stored upon arrival at the distribution center, depot and/or trial site. Should address expectations for adequate and safe receipt, handl

artifacts.name: IP Sample Label
artifacts.definition: A sample of each IP label type (for every pack and every language) to be used in the trial; approval status must be clear. All stages of label text development are included within this artifact.

artifacts.name: IP Shipment Documentation
artifacts.definition: To record details of the shipment process including approval , requests, dispatch, tracking and receipts to/from a distribution center, depot and/or trial site.

artifacts.name: IP Accountability Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document records of the allocation of IP to/from a distribution center, depot, trial site and/or site to subject and the reconciliation of IP prior to return to the sponsor.

artifacts.name: IP Transfer Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the process and approval for the transfer of IP from one depot to another depot and/or from one site to another site (within or across protocols). Examples include sponsor approval for tra

artifacts.name: IP Re-labeling Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the plan for the re-labeling process to occur at the distribution center, depot and/or site and confirmation records that the re-labeling occurred.

artifacts.name: IP Recall Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the plan for the recall process for the IP to occur at a distribution center, depot and/or site; will include confirmation records that the recall occurred.

artifacts.name: IP Quality Complaint Form
artifacts.definition: To document or record an IP quality complaint.

artifacts.name: IP Return Documentation
artifacts.definition: To record details of returns to/from a distribution center, depot and/or trial site.  Examples include courier documentation and packing/ inventory listing.

artifacts.name: IP Certificate of Destruction
artifacts.definition: To document the confirmation of destruction of IP at the end of a trial at a distribution center, depot and/or site .

artifacts.name: IP Retest and Expiry Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the batch retesting/analyses of IP for a variety of reasons such as stability confirmation and expiry extension

artifacts.name: QP (Qualified Person) Certification
artifacts.definition: To confirm that any IP from another country has been manufactured and checked in accordance with standards of Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) at least equivalent to those laid down in Directive 91/

artifacts.name: IP Regulatory Release Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the regulatory IP release process.

artifacts.name: IP Verification Statements
artifacts.definition: Any certificate, license or other documentation that is required by a specific regulation to verify the quality, source, manufacture, ingredients or other aspect of investigational and/or control prod

artifacts.name: Certificate of Analysis
artifacts.definition: To document identity, purity and strength of the IP(s) to be used trial, in accordance with the specifications of the IP, including the acceptance limits and the actual results of the tests.

artifacts.name: IP Treatment Allocation Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the treatment allocation or device serial numbers for each subject; used if urgent unblinding or code break is needed or when interim or final unblinding occurs.

artifacts.name: IP Unblinding Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the plan and procedures to be taken should the action of breaking the blind for an individual subject be urgently needed, or when interim or final unblinding occurs.

artifacts.name: IP Treatment Decoding Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the action of breaking the blind for an individual subject, urgently if needed, or when interim or final unblinding occurs. Treatment unblinding may be controlled by interactive response t

artifacts.name: IP Storage Condition Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the unique storage conditions of the IP at the sponsor (if sponsor is distributing), distribution center, depot, trial site and in transit, if required by the available stability requireme

artifacts.name: IP Storage Condition Excursion Documentation
artifacts.definition: To record excursions for IP, from the acceptable pre-defined condition range either during transit or storage at a distribution center, depot and/or trial site.

artifacts.name: Maintenance Logs
artifacts.definition: To record activities and times when quality of condition of IP, Non-IP, device and other trial supplies is assessed over period of use and any maintenance performed, including software logs and certif

artifacts.name: Non-IP Supply Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the details and quantity of non-IP supplies needed to fulfill the trial protocol requirements over the life of the trial. May include but is not limited to, supplementary medication, pre-t

artifacts.name: Non-IP Shipment Documentation
artifacts.definition: To record details of the shipment of non-IP supplies needed to fulfill the trial protocol requirements to a distribution center, depot and/or site.

artifacts.name: Non-IP Return Documentation
artifacts.definition: To inventory the returns of certain non-IP supplies needed to fulfill the trial protocol requirements to a distribution center, depot and/or site. Examples include courier documentation and packing/ i

artifacts.name: Non-IP Storage Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document the unique storage conditions of the Non-IP supplies at the sponsor (if sponsor is distributing), distribution center, depot, trial site and in transit, if required by the available stabil

artifacts.name: IRT User Requirement Specification
artifacts.definition: To document end user requirements from design and capabilities of an interactive response technology (IRT) such as Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS) or Interactive Web Response System (IWRS), i

artifacts.name: IRT Validation Certification
artifacts.definition: To confirm the validation status of the interactive response technology (IRT).

artifacts.name: IRT User Acceptance Testing (UAT) Certification
artifacts.definition: To document the acceptability of the series of assessments of the IRT performed by key users of the system that are designed to show that the IRT has been correctly programmed and meets the requiremen

artifacts.name: IRT User Manual
artifacts.definition: To provide instructions and define the operational instructions for the IRT for the user.

artifacts.name: IRT User Account Management
artifacts.definition: To capture account management details for all users who received access to the system; should include security role, data account granted, date account disabled

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Safety Management Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the end-to-end process for the ongoing safety evaluation for the investigational product; includes data to be collected, reporting objectives and processes for a clinical trial. Plan may i

artifacts.name: Pharmacovigilance Database Line Listing
artifacts.definition: Listing of trial data for a single study trial used for a variety of safety evaluation of the investigational product purposes (e.g. Serious Adverse Events (SAE) case listings, database line listings,

artifacts.name: Expedited Safety Report
artifacts.definition: To document unexpected serious adverse drug reactions and other safety information; submitted to regulatory authorities and IRBs/IECs.  Submission artifact is located in 03.03.01.

artifacts.name: SAE Report
artifacts.definition: To organize critical data around a serious adverse event, adverse event and/or a laboratory abnormality as identified in the protocol. Reports may include, but are not limited to: specific investigato

artifacts.name: Pregnancy Report
artifacts.definition: To organize critical data around a pregnancy that occurred whilst either the male or the female subject was participating in a clinical trial. Reporting forms and supporting data collected for pregnan

artifacts.name: Special Events of Interest
artifacts.definition: To organize critical data around a special event of interest, one that is of scientific and medical concern specific to the product or program. Usually requested by or submitted to Regulatory Agencies

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific, trial specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to:

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific, trial specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.  (Does not include program level tracking information.)

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related, trial specific meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Certification or Accreditation
artifacts.definition: To document recognition and approval by an authorized accrediting body applying known acceptable standards, that the central or local facility is competent to perform required test(s) and support reli

artifacts.name: Laboratory Validation Documentation
artifacts.definition: To document through use of control data that a central or local laboratory can consistently and reproducibly report results that are reliable; may include but is not limited to reporting of calibratio

artifacts.name: Laboratory Results Documentation
artifacts.definition: Summary listings or individual subject reports provided by the central or local laboratory or other testing facility, e.g. results of biochemical testing, histological examination.

artifacts.name: Normal Ranges
artifacts.definition: To define acceptable limits (where 95% of the population that a central or local facility serves will fall) for comparative interpretation that allow for medical decisions to be made; may be included

artifacts.name: Manual
artifacts.definition: To outline the procedures to be followed in the collection, handling and shipping of samples; may not be available for local facilities.

artifacts.name: Supply Import Documentation
artifacts.definition: To provide the necessary documentation required per country to allow for importation of supplies (non-drug / IP), may also include biological samples and related test material (kits, etc.)

artifacts.name: Head of Facility Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To verify that the Head of Facility is suitably qualified to lead and oversee the management and reporting of results; may be included with Certification / Accreditation; may be found in the User Manu

artifacts.name: Standardization Methods
artifacts.definition: To confirm that two or more central or local facilities can perform the same test / procedure and obtain consistent results; includes but may not be limited to cross-calibration of test methods betwee

artifacts.name: Specimen Label
artifacts.definition: To capture critical information about the collection of a sample; may include but is not limited to subject ID, date and time of collection, etc.; may be included in User Manual.

artifacts.name: Shipment Records
artifacts.definition: To provide relevant details for samples sent in any one shipment.

artifacts.name: Sample Storage Condition Log
artifacts.definition: To monitor and track sample storage under the appropriate conditions.

artifacts.name: Sample Import or Export Documentation
artifacts.definition: To provide the necessary documentation required per country to allow for importation/exportation of samples.

artifacts.name: Record of Retained Samples
artifacts.definition: To document location and identification of body fluid, tissue samples or genetic samples being held for possible future (re)testing; to include destruction records, when and if this occurs.

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Qualification and Compliance
artifacts.definition: To confirm that a third party meets all relevant criteria to fulfill a contractual obligation; may include a quality questionnaire, a visit report to qualify their capabilities, other documents that s

artifacts.name: Third Party Curriculum Vitae
artifacts.definition: To document qualifications and eligibility of Individual Third Party Trial Team Members; CVs, Questionnaires etc. including translators, for the project. Not intended to duplicate records filed in Zon

artifacts.name: Ongoing Third Party Oversight
artifacts.definition: To confirm throughout the duration of a study that a third party continues to meet all relevant criteria to fulfill a contractual obligation.

artifacts.name: Confidentiality Agreement
artifacts.definition: To confirm by written legal agreement that key information between parties will be prevented from being inappropriately disclosed. May be included in another contractual agreement.

artifacts.name: Vendor Selection
artifacts.definition: To identify how a third party was selected. May include details of other third parties short-listed, master vendor list and any assessments carried out prior to selection.

artifacts.name: Contractual Agreement
artifacts.definition: To document by a written dated signed agreement between two or more parties that defines any arrangements on delegation and distribution of tasks and obligations (including financial obligations); cri

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Data Management Plan
artifacts.definition: To identify the overall strategy for data management process for the trial; a compilation of documents that may include amendments/appendices but are not limited to: Completion Guidelines, Data Qualit

artifacts.name: CRF Completion Requirements
artifacts.definition: To provide detailed instructions on how data points on each CRF are to be completed; how to enter on paper and if EDC, how to enter data into the system.

artifacts.name: Annotated CRF
artifacts.definition: To assign variable names and attributes to the fields on the CRF and to link the variables to the tables within the database; may also be used as an aid for database programming on how to structure th

artifacts.name: Documentation of Corrections to Entered Data
artifacts.definition: Any documentation used to query database discrepancies and to record approved corrections to the clinical trial database; may include self-evident corrections, global queries, SAE queries, laboratory

artifacts.name: Final Subject Data
artifacts.definition: Final Subject data (EDC/ePRO/Paper) for the protocol and a copy of each site's data by-subject. Associated documents may include but are not limited to documentation of subject data corrections, subje

artifacts.name: Database  Requirements
artifacts.definition: To provide a detailed design framework for the system(s) used to manage and store subject/patient data captured via a paper CRF or eCRF for the specified trial. Not to be confused with specifications

artifacts.name: Edit Check Plan
artifacts.definition: Specifications which will detect data that is illogical, unexpected, missing, redundant, or is outside of defined study parameters; usually implemented via programming logic

artifacts.name: Edit Check Programming
artifacts.definition: The computer code which satisfies the edit check plan/specification details; may include a reference to where the code resides.

artifacts.name: Edit Check Testing
artifacts.definition: To provide evidence that the data edit checks have been implemented correctly; can include the data used to test the programming logic

artifacts.name: Approval for Database Activation
artifacts.definition: Documentation that all database specification requirements have been satisfied and system can go live; will also include confirmation that UAT (user acceptance testing) has been successfully completed

artifacts.name: External Data Transfer Specifications
artifacts.definition: To document import and export data specifications; includes but is not limited to diary, lab, IVRS, imaging; integration from external systems to database and may include transfer from one group to an

artifacts.name: Data Entry Guidelines (Paper)
artifacts.definition: To provide detailed instructions on how CRF data is to be entered into a database; specific to a paper CRF trial (therefore, would not be required with an EDC trial).

artifacts.name: SAE Reconciliation
artifacts.definition: To document reconciliation and resolution of discrepancies between the SAEs in the safety and the clinical databases has been successfully completed.

artifacts.name: Dictionary Coding
artifacts.definition: To document the tools used in medical coding and the final coded terms; includes medical sign off of coding; may include resolution discrepancies.

artifacts.name: Data Review Documentation
artifacts.definition: To describe the procedures for creating and implementing a Quality Control (QC) Plan or Data Review Plan to ensure that quality data is captured into a clinical database on an ongoing basis. Artifact

artifacts.name: Database Lock and Unlock Approval
artifacts.definition: Confirmation that all of the requirements for database release have been meet; may include all unlock and re-lock documentation as well as a report on data quality issues and summary of essential acti

artifacts.name: Database Change Control
artifacts.definition: Summary of requested change, reason for change, relevant approvals, impact / risk analysis, associated requirements, specifications and other documentation describing the validation and implementation

artifacts.name: System Account Management
artifacts.definition: To capture account management details for all users who received access to the system (e.g.: ePRO, eCRF); intended to include users' security role, date account granted, date account disabled.

artifacts.name: Technical Design Document
artifacts.definition: A technical planning and tracking document containing all the elements required to build and test the EDC application including the variables to be collected, their logical arrangement, navigation bet

artifacts.name: Validation Documentation
artifacts.definition: Documents establishing the project context and documentation requirements for EDC (e.g.: eCRF or ePRO); can include the plan for and results of, the user acceptance testing (UAT). Includes the validat

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.

artifacts.name: Statistical Analysis Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the statistical aspects of the trial design, the process of data selection for all analyzes, the data items to be analyzed and all the procedures and methods to be employed in the analysis

artifacts.name: Sample Size Calculation
artifacts.definition: To document the technique, assumptions and output used to calculate the sample size; can include QC and sign off.

artifacts.name: Randomization Plan
artifacts.definition: To describe the randomization scheme (e.g. number and name of treatments, strata, block size) and how the randomization will be carried out; this plan is then used to initiate programming.

artifacts.name: Randomization Procedure
artifacts.definition: To define the actual steps for how subjects are randomized in a trial. This could be by interactive response technology (IRT)/IVRS, or a manual process ((i.e. work instruction). May be part of the ran

artifacts.name: Master Randomization List
artifacts.definition: The single source on the assignment of subjects to protocol specified groups.  In blinded studies, this list remains blinded until its release following the final data lock.

artifacts.name: Randomization Programming
artifacts.definition: Computer code to generate randomization number for treatment assignment.

artifacts.name: Randomization Sign Off
artifacts.definition: To verify that the randomization program generates the randomization number and treatment assignment correctly according to the randomization schema specified for the trial.

artifacts.name: End of Trial or Interim Unblinding
artifacts.definition: To document and authorize the release of the randomization code and allow the trial data to be unblinded. Includes evidence of release of end of trial unblinding. May include a request for partial unb

artifacts.name: Data Definitions for Analysis Datasets
artifacts.definition: To define the programming logic required to transform the raw dataset to the analysis dataset; includes populations, etc.; as outlined in the SAP.

artifacts.name: Analysis QC Documentation
artifacts.definition: To confirm the QC procedures for analysis programs and validation of analysis QC programs, as well as the actual output of the QC steps.

artifacts.name: Interim Analysis Raw Datasets
artifacts.definition: The export of raw data for interim analysis purposes. This may include CDISC datasets such as Operational Data Model (ODM) or SDTM.

artifacts.name: Interim Analysis Programs
artifacts.definition: The suite of programs designed to generate the interim analysis outputs as referenced in the SAP.

artifacts.name: Interim Analysis Datasets
artifacts.definition: The datasets used for the interim analyses.

artifacts.name: Interim Analysis Output
artifacts.definition: The Tables Listings and Figures produced from the interim analysis datasets; includes Statistics approval.

artifacts.name: Final Analysis Raw Datasets
artifacts.definition: The export of raw data for final analysis purposes. This may include CDISC datasets such as Operational Data Model (ODM), SDTM or case report tabulation (CRT) package

artifacts.name: Final Analysis Programs
artifacts.definition: The suite of programs designed to generate the final analysis outputs as referenced in the SAP.

artifacts.name: Final Analysis Datasets
artifacts.definition: The datasets used for the final analysis/case report tabulation (CRT) package. If required, study-level submission datasets can be filed here.

artifacts.name: Final Analysis Output
artifacts.definition: The Tables, Listings and Figures produced from the final analysis datasets; includes Statistics approval. May be appended to the CSR.

artifacts.name: Subject Evaluability Criteria and Subject Classification
artifacts.definition: To document the decisions which define the criteria applied to evaluate each subject in the trial, in order to that will unambiguously assign the subject to the populations established in the SAP.

artifacts.name: Interim Statistical Report(s)
artifacts.definition: To summarize the relevant statistical aspects of the interim analysis. May be appended to the CSR.

artifacts.name: Statistical Report
artifacts.definition: To summarize the relevant statistical aspects of the final analysis. May be appended to the CSR.

artifacts.name: Relevant Communications
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific agreements, significant discussions or relevant information, but not specifically listed in this Reference Model. Types of correspondence may include, but not limited to: letters, memo,

artifacts.name: Tracking Information
artifacts.definition: Zone-specific documents developed for the purpose of tracking activities during the course of the trial.

artifacts.name: Meeting Material
artifacts.definition: Agenda, presentation materials and other documentation generated during an internal or external zone-related meeting which documents any agreements or significant discussions. Includes meeting minutes

artifacts.name: Filenote
artifacts.definition: To document any decision or to clarify any information relating to this zone.
